methodology_notes: Public 2026 CISO budget/priorities survey from Glilot Capital (Israeli cyber VC; helps ground non-US / Israel-network CISO views). Participants include security leaders from Blackstone, Virgin, NTT, Rakuten, AppLovin, Forter, Nebius and others. Sample size not disclosed. Strong, current AI-investment + vendor-strategy signal.

distributions.context: 77.8% of CISOs plan to invest in AI-powered cybersecurity tools; ~80% cite AI-related solutions as high-priority 2026 funding.
41.3% intend to allocate budget for AI systems designed for task automation.
34.9% prefer best product per category, 33.3% aim to reduce vendors, only 6.3% favor relying on 2–3 broad platforms. Reinforces that 'consolidation' is contested, not consensus.
58.7% believe operational use of AI for defense will become standard by end of 2026; 55.6% flag securing AI-generated code, 50.8% detecting AI-driven attacks, 47.6% AI governance/monitoring.
Cloud data protection and identity threat detection were each favored by ~33% of respondents as investment areas.
